{{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.cdnfonts.com/css/barcade'); .page { font-family: 'MS Pgothic'; } .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: #00004d; --theme-page-background-color--secondary: #00004d; --theme-page-text-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-community-header-color: #5353b5; --theme-link-color: #3d3dff; --theme-accent-label-color: #7370ff; --theme-accent-color: #1f26ab; --theme-border-color: #020063; --theme-body-background-color: #00004d; --theme-body-background-image: none; } }} {{Audio|=true|file=Blackfairy.mp3|align=center|loop=true|volume=0.3|secrettext=...?}} || OVERVIEW || '''Thoughtformation''' is a very rare, wish-fulfilling phenomenon, that has only ever been documented a handful of times. '''Thoughtformation''', or '''Phenomenon 9-A,''' is a phenomenon where depending on the state of mind of a wanderer, their will can bleed into reality, creating a manifestation of their desire. Any matter that may be created from this phenomenon originates from [[The Broken]] and its manifestations can take on many forms depending on the interpretation of the affected wanderer's thoughts, similar to a prophecy of sorts. A determined wanderer on the brink of death from walking the winding roads of [[Level 91]], in a moment of desperation, may unintentionally manifest a bottle of [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. But occasionally, the phenomenon could instead cruelly summon a hound in the face of a wanderer wishing to no longer be alone. '''Phenomenon 9-A''' is largely thought to be entirely random in when it occurs, but there have been wanderers and supposed experts in the past who claimed to be able to harness the phenomenon as their own, and even control how their constructs were interpreted and implemented. However, most claims of control over '''Thoughtformation''' have eventually been disproven or have otherwise remained unconfirmed. '''Thoughtformation''' has close echoes to [[The Kauer Thesis]], but the phenomenon can never affect anyone within the [[The Frontrooms]], due to its reliance on psychic energy that cannot permeate through the void. Its rare and often localized effects have led to '''Phenomenon 9-A's''' classification as a variant of [[Phenomenon 9|Tulpaefication]], but while Tulpaefication can create rather powerful entities with the collective belief of many, the upper limits of what '''Phenomenon 9-A''' is capable of are not fully understood. Its ability to manifest in many different ways, and on a variety of scales, makes the potential of this phenomenon seemingly endless. || CAUSES || Similarly to [[Phenomenon 9]], [[Achospic_Plane|psychic radiation]] is the main component to '''Thoughtformation'''. While '''Tulpaefication''' is a passive effect of '''psychic radiation''' build-ups within the [[Achospic Plane]], '''Phenomenon 9-A''' acts as an active effect of build-ups that occur when a large amount of psychic radiation is suddenly dumped in a place that already has an excess of it. This excess energy is quickly expended in order to return the balance, which triggers a chain reaction leading to a '''Thoughtformation''' event. This makes the phenomenon quite rare, due to psychic energy often spreading evenly throughout the backrooms and '''Achospic Plane''', making the conditions that must be met for a '''Thoughtformation''' event—very situational. || CASES || While the phenomenon is exceedingly rare, there are still many profound cases and tales of '''Thoughtformation''', and it is even been theorized that [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]] was caused by a major '''Thoughtformation''' event triggered by a desperate researcher within [[Level 6.5]] On the day of April 17th, 2023, [[Backrooms Today]] published a feature detailing the story of a woman going by the moniker, "Rosemord." Word quickly went around of the wanderer who had become the only survivor among 75 victims after miraculously escaping the [[Red Rooms]]. This feature, and Rosemord's account of the level, would prove invaluable in the research of the '''Red Rooms''', except for one detail that was amiss. The woman hadn't actually found an exit-rather in a moment of vitality and will, she had created one. In an interview conducted by Scut Wakante, Rosemord detailed her experience within the Red Rooms. Scut Wakante : ''"How did you exit the Red Rooms, it's a dead-end is it not?"'' Scut Wakante : ''"Do you have any idea what happened leading up to your escape?"'' Rosemord : ''"My memory was fuzzy for a while after I left, but I think now I can recall most of it."'' Scut Wakante : ''"Take your time, Ms. Rose."'' Rosemord : ''"Hmph. . .I remember being very thirsty; coulda' drank a river. I was slouched over on the ground, just waiting for it to be over, when I saw an older faceling boy standin' right in front-a me." Scut Wakante : ''"This faceling, did it seem familiar?"'' Rosemord : ''"Hell if I know. He didn't seem threatening-he just. . .looked down at me, like he felt bad. He offered his hand, and I had to muster all the strength I had left to take it."'' Rosemord : ''"When I did, I felt this rush of energy and I woke up. I practically ripped my hair off the carpet as I sprang up, and I just started runnin'. I don't know where I was runnin'. I scraped up my arms, my legs—my whole body, as I pushed myself through the holes in those walls over and over."'' Rosemord : ''"Every time I felt like stopping, I imagined the door was just around the corner, just one more room, one more crack. But the last thing I expected, was to eventually turn that corner again, and see it."'' It is thought that within the small area of the '''Red Rooms''', by concentrating on the idea of the door, a successful interpretation of Rosemord's exit was created, allowing her to leave. But due to the localization of the event, the thoughtform was quickly dispelled once Rosemord exited, making her the only wanderer to ever be able to exit the Red Rooms. Ms. Rose has since made a full recovery and now resides in a colony within [[Level 10]].

''Recovered Log''

Date: February 1, 2016 [Slow, labored breathing, background static noise] Alex: This is Alex Hoffman. Recording. . .My observations of [[Level 6.5]]. This is my second day. My recording equipment and other electronics are beginning to fail due to excess electromagnetic radiation. Alex: I have. . . [GAG, VOMITING] Alex: ''Fuck!'' Alex: . . .I'm fearful that if I do not find a way out of this place, I will soon die. [QUICK BREATH] Alex: It's getting hot, I've drawn too much attention to myself... Alex: The walls have changed again, I can't tell where I am... [APPROACHING FLAMES, HYPERVENTILATING] Alex: No, no, no. [THUMP] [BANG] Alex: That's the door right? Where's the handle? Alex: I can't see! No! I should've never left the room! I don't want to die! Alex: It's too bright! Make it stop! Please! [BANG, SCRATCHING] Alex: No! No! [QUICK PULSATING] Alex: No! [GARBAGE NOISE] [BANG] Alex: NO! [THUD, LOUD STATIC]
[Sound of heavy breathing and digging through a bag] Alex: It's dark, I appear to have exited back to [[Level 6]], but the floor is carpeted, am I in another sublevel? [CLICK] Alex: Wait. This is [[Level 4]]. Alex: So why is it so. . .

Dark?
